mnemonics: I D-D'ed the Data: Use Insert, then Delete, then Display to remember the order of operations for managing a list.
keyPoints: Insertion requires finding the correct position and shifting elements to make space.
Deletion requires finding the target element and shifting elements to fill the gap.
Display traverses the structure from the beginning to the end, accessing each element.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to update pointers/references or the 'size' counter after an insertion or deletion.
Attempting to delete from or insert into an empty data structure, leading to errors.
Not checking the bounds (e.g., index out of range) before performing an insertion or deletion operation.
